briefdescription.para: This main function starts the USB application.
This is the USB initialization function.

detaileddescription.para:
This example initializes the device for Mass Storage Application. The following configuration is done.
EP0 : CONTROL end point, Bidirectional, Packet size 64 bytes.
EP1 : NON_ISOCHRONOUS, BULK_IN, packet size 512 bytes.
EP2 : NON_ISOCHRONOUS, BULK_OUT, packet size 512 bytes

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

briefdescription.para: This function processes the reset and disconnect conditions of the USB mass storage device.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB mass storage device application.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from to the function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function processes the USB End point Zero events.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point Zero events.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the caller function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

simplesect.para: EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point zero. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function processes the USB End point one events.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point one events.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the caller function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

simplesect.para: EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point one. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function processes the USB End point two events.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point two events.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the caller function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

simplesect.para: EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point two. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function processes mass storage specific commands and sends the required response.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

briefdescription.para: This function implements the transmission of data from the device to the READ request from the USB Host.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is a pointer to the Mass Storage Command Block wrapper.
is a pointer to the Mass Storage Status wrapper.

briefdescription.para: This function implements the reception of data in the USB device for the write request from the USB Host.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is a pointer to the Mass Storage Command Block wrapper.
is a pointer to the Mass Storage Status wrapper.

briefdescription.para: This routine is called when a RESET class command is received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance of the controller.

briefdescription.para: This routine is called when a GETMAXLUN class command is received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance of the controller.

briefdescription.para: This file contains Mass storage device application related functions implemented in polled mode.

simplesect.para: The example is tested on MicroBlaze, with caches included in the H/W design. This example works for USB high speed interface only.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
4.00a hvm 05/24/11 Created based on the xusb_storage.c example. 4.01a bss 11/01/11 Modified UsbIfIntrHandler function to unconditionally reset when USB reset is asserted (CR 627574). 4.02a bss 04/05/12 Modified the sequence of Cache Flush and Invalidation. The cache flush happens just before the driver API EPDataSend is called. Similarly the cache invalidation is done after the call to EPDataReceive and after the dma transfer is over. 5.6 pm 07/05/23 Removed powerpc support. 5.6 pm 07/05/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.
